flashcards.q: When was the Delhi Sultanate established?
Which King was defeated to establish the Delhi Sultanate?
Name the five dynasties of the Delhi Sultanate.
flashcards.a: Mamluks, Khiljis, Tughlaqs, Sayyids, and Lodis.

flashcards.q: Who was 'Sikander Sani'?
Who was Malik Kafur?
flashcards.a: Ala-ud-din Khilji's slave-general who expanded the Sultanate to the South.

flashcards.q: Where did Muhammad bin Tughlaq move his capital to from Delhi?
What is 'Token Currency' in the Tughlaq context?
flashcards.a: Declaring cheap copper coins to have the value of silver or gold.

flashcards.q: Who invaded Delhi in 1398, leaving it in ruins?
Who built the Sun Temple at Konark?
flashcards.a: King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga kingdom.

flashcards.q: Which fortress in Rajasthan has the world's second longest wall?
Who founded the Vijayanagara Empire?
What is the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire?
Who is the most celebrated ruler of Vijayanagara?
Which battle marked the end of the Vijayanagara city in 1565?
In which battle did Babur establish the Mughal Empire?
What are the two key military technologies Babur introduced?
Who ruled Delhi briefly as Hemchandra Vikramaditya?
flashcards.a: Himu (or Hemu), a general under the Suri rulers.

flashcards.q: Who won the Second Battle of Panipat in 1556?
What is 'Jauhar'?
flashcards.a: A heroic act of final resistance where women committed mass self-immolation to avoid capture.

flashcards.q: Define 'Sulh-i-kul'.
flashcards.a: Akbar's doctrine of 'peace with all' or tolerance of all faiths.

flashcards.q: Name the major Sanskrit text translated into Persian as 'Razmnama'.
Who built the Taj Mahal?
Under which emperor did the Mughal Empire reach its greatest territorial extent?
What tax did Aurangzeb reinstate that Akbar had abolished?
Where did the Ahom kingdom originate from?
flashcards.a: Present-day Myanmar (migrated to Brahmaputra Valley in the 13th century).

flashcards.q: Who was the legendary Ahom commander at the Battle of Saraighat?
Which Sikh Guru established the 'Khalsa'?
Who unified the Sikh confederacies into a strong empire in the 19th century?
Compare Iqta vs. Mansabdari systems.
flashcards.a: Iqta: Land assignments for revenue collection (Sultanate). Mansabdari: Rank-based military system with jagirs (Mughal).

flashcards.q: What is a 'Hundi'?
flashcards.a: A traditional written financial instrument for transferring funds across distances (precursor to banking).
